name: Chocolate-Dipped Coconut Shortbread
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces semisweet chocolate chips, MELTED WITH
recipeInstructions:
1. Buttery shortbread laced with coconut and dipped into dark chocolate.
2. Combine flour, baking powder, salt; set aside.
3. Beat together butter, sugar, and vanilla until light and fluffy.
4. Stir in the coconut, four 1/2 cup at a time, and mix until well-blended.
5. Form dough into a 4-inch by 7-inch rectangle. Wrap and refrigerate until firm, about 2 hours.
6. Preheat oven to 300°F.
7. Butter two large baking sheets.
8. On a floured surface, roll the dough into an 8-by-14-inch rectangle, about 1/4 inch thick.
9. Using a floured knife with a long blade, cut into 24, 2-inch squares.
10. Arrange the squares, 1 inch apart, on buttered sheets.
11. Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden.
12. Cool on racks.
13. Line two large baking sheets with waxed paper and set aside.
14. Melt chocolate and butter together, stirring to blend well.
15. Dip half of each cookie diagonally into the chocolate, shaking off excess.
16. Arrange on paper-lined sheets.
17. Refrigerate until the chocolate sets, about 10 minutes.
18. Store airtight at room temperature or freeze for longer storage.
